skill.name: playwright-cli
skill.description: Guides AI agents through browser automation with playwright-cli for navigation, testing, screenshots, storage management, tracing, and generated Playwright test steps.
skill.summary: Automate web testing and browser workflows through the Playwright CLI.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: The static command findings are mostly false positives from markdown command examples, not Ruby backtick execution. The skill is still medium risk because it intentionally allows browser automation, arbitrary Playwright page code, network navigation, storage-state files, persistent profiles, and trace artifacts that can expose sensitive session data. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration path was found.

medium_findings.title: Browser Storage State Can Expose Sessions
medium_findings.description: The skill documents saving, loading, listing, and editing cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage, and authentication state files. This is legitimate for testing, but saved state can contain session tokens and must be protected.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly show state-save, state-load, cookie commands, and security notes about auth tokens. The context is legitimate automation, so the risk is exposure through misuse rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Playwright Code Execution in Browser Context
medium_findings.description: The skill supports run-code and eval commands that execute user supplied Playwright or JavaScript snippets in the active browser session. This enables advanced testing, but it can read page content, grant browser permissions, and manipulate storage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly documented and the examples show page evaluation and permission changes. The capability is expected for a Playwright CLI skill, but it increases impact if misused.

medium_findings.title: Trace and Video Artifacts May Capture Sensitive Data
medium_findings.description: Tracing records DOM snapshots, screenshots, network requests, headers, bodies, and console logs. Video recording and traces can preserve secrets typed during automated flows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reference states that traces capture network bodies, DOM snapshots, screenshots, and checkout form inputs. This is strong evidence of sensitive artifact risk without evidence of automatic exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Browser Profiles Retain Local Data
medium_findings.description: The skill supports persistent sessions and custom profile directories. Persistent profiles can keep cookies, local storage, and other browser data beyond the immediate automation task.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands explicitly create persistent sessions and provide cleanup commands. The risk is manageable, but users must understand retention and delete data after sensitive workflows.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick detections occur in fenced markdown blocks that show playwright-cli usage. They are not Ruby backtick execution in source code, although the skill intentionally allows the playwright-cli command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown command examples, not executable Ruby code. The allowed Bash wrapper is real, but the specific static Ruby backtick label is a false positive.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Example Targets
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings use documentation domains such as example.com and playwright.dev. I did not find evidence that these URLs receive secrets or hidden telemetry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL examples are clearly part of user-facing command documentation and test workflows. No evidence found of a fixed third-party collection endpoint.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: No reviewed file contained suspicious override language, fake system instructions, pre-approval claims, or requests to skip analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review of the skill and references found no prompt injection phrases. The confidence is high, but this finding is limited to the files included in the audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Boundary Allows Playwright CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for playwright-cli commands. This is necessary for the skill, but it means the agent can launch browsers, write artifacts, and perform web interactions through the CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command list are explicit. The scope is restricted to playwright-cli, so this is a managed but meaningful capability.

dangerous_patterns.title: run-code Provides Custom Automation Logic
dangerous_patterns.description: The run-code examples execute custom Playwright functions, including permissions, clipboard access, page content reading, and storage manipulation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples show real Playwright APIs that read or change browser state. They are legitimate examples, but users should review generated actions before use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authentication Artifacts Are Written to Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The references show auth state saved to JSON files and trace files written under the Playwright CLI artifact directory. These files can contain sensitive page and session data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited documentation directly describes saved auth state and trace files. The risk is local exposure or accidental commit, not evidence of automatic leakage.

content.user_title: Automate Browser Testing with Playwright CLI
content.value_statement: Browser testing often needs repeatable navigation, inspection, screenshots, and storage setup. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured playwright-cli workflows for those tasks.
actual_capabilities: Open browser sessions, navigate pages, and interact with elements by snapshot references.
Fill forms, click controls, press keys, upload files, and manage tabs.
Capture screenshots, PDFs, traces, videos, console output, and network activity.
Save, load, list, edit, and clear cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage, and auth state.
Mock or modify network requests through route commands and custom Playwright code.
Generate Playwright TypeScript actions from interactive browser sessions.
limitations: It requires playwright-cli to be installed or available through npx.
Generated tests still need human assertions and cleanup logic.
Storage state, traces, screenshots, and videos may contain sensitive data.
Advanced run-code snippets can fail if page structure or permissions change.

use_cases.title: Debug a Web App Flow
use_cases.description: Reproduce a user journey, inspect snapshots, capture console output, and record traces for failed interactions.

use_cases.title: Generate Test Steps
use_cases.description: Interact with a page and convert the session into Playwright actions that can become an automated test.

use_cases.title: Prepare Authenticated Browser State
use_cases.description: Log in once, save browser state, and reuse that state for later automation in the same trusted project.

prompt_templates.title: Open and Inspect a Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use playwright-cli to open this URL, take a snapshot, and summarize the visible interactive elements: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Automate a Form Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use playwright-cli to complete this form flow in a test environment. Stop before submitting if sensitive data appears: [steps].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Playwright Test Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate through this user journey with playwright-cli and return a concise Playwright test outline with recommended assertions: [journey].

prompt_templates.title: Debug Network and State Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Use playwright-cli tracing and network tools to diagnose this failing web flow. Avoid saving secrets and list any risky artifacts created: [flow].

output_examples.input: Inspect the login page and identify form fields.
output_examples.output: A short summary of visible controls, stable element references, likely email and password fields, and the next safe action.

output_examples.input: Record a checkout failure for debugging.
output: Failure reproduced at the payment step.
Artifacts created locally and flagged as sensitive.

output_examples.input: Turn an interaction into a Playwright test draft.
output_examples.output: A readable test outline with navigation, form actions, click steps, and suggested assertions to add manually.

best_practices: Use test accounts and non-production data when saving storage state or traces.
Delete screenshots, videos, traces, and auth files after they are no longer needed.
Review generated Playwright actions and add assertions before committing tests.
anti_patterns: Do not commit storage state files, traces, videos, or screenshots that include secrets.
Do not use persistent profiles for sensitive sessions unless cleanup is planned.
Do not run custom page code on untrusted sites without reviewing the requested actions.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It automates browser navigation, page interaction, screenshots, traces, videos, storage state, request mocking, and Playwright test step generation.

faq.question: Does it require Playwright?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill expects playwright-cli to be available globally or through npx when a local install is needed.

faq.question: Can it generate complete tests?
faq.answer: It can generate useful Playwright action steps. Humans should add assertions, fixtures, cleanup, and stable test data.

faq.question: Is it safe with authenticated sessions?
faq.answer: It can be safe in trusted projects, but storage state files may contain tokens. Keep them out of version control.

faq.question: Can it mock network requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports route commands and custom Playwright code for mocking, modifying, blocking, or delaying requests.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill has legitimate powerful browser capabilities. It can handle sensitive storage, traces, network data, and custom page code.
